source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 16.2.1 rsyslog configuration (pp. 304-307)

questions.q: What is the MAIN rsyslog configuration file?
questions.why: /etc/rsyslog.conf is the main file; /etc/rsyslog.d/ holds additional drop-in configs.

questions.q: Which directory holds additional rsyslog configuration files?
questions.why: /etc/rsyslog.d/ is the drop-in directory for extra config snippets.

questions.q: What are the three parts of /etc/rsyslog.conf?
options: Load module, Global Directives, and Rules
questions.why: rsyslog.conf consists of module loading, global directives, and the rules section.

questions.q: Which rsyslog configuration format is deprecated and should not be used?
questions.why: rsyslog supports basic (sysklogd), advanced (RainerScript), and the deprecated legacy format - avoid the last.
